description_long: This house features a modern, rectilinear design with a predominantly white exterior and extensive use of large, floor-to-ceiling glass windows and doors. The structure is composed of multiple interconnected rectangular volumes, creating a dynamic and layered appearance. The front includes a double garage with a segmented door and a prominent glass entryway, while the rear and sides showcase expansive glass facades that likely open to living spaces or a patio. The roof is flat throughout, emphasizing the contemporary architectural style. Window arrangements are asymmetrical, with some sections featuring grouped panes and others single, large panels. The overall impression is of a spacious, light-filled residence with a minimalist aesthetic and strong indoor-outdoor connectivity.
description: A modern, white house with flat roofs, large glass windows, and a double garage, featuring a minimalist, rectilinear design and asymmetrical window placement.
description_view_0: This view highlights the side of the house with a large glass wall and a smaller section with a decorative window, showing the layered structure.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the rear of the house, dominated by a wide expanse of floor-to-ceiling glass and a clear view into the open-plan interior.
description_view_2: This side view displays the interplay of solid white walls and multiple window types, including grouped and single panes, as well as the protruding glass section.
description_view_3: The front view reveals the main entryway, double garage, and a central glass facade above the entrance, providing a welcoming and modern appearance.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows and grouped panes
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a glass door, wide steps, and a double garage with a segmented door, all framed by clean, minimalist lines.
